title: Kaboom Crew MVP 1 polish — audio, particles, score, title, difficulty

stories.title: Engine audio primitive — load + play + stop sound effects
stories.summary: Tiny engine-level audio surface under `engine/runtime/audio/`. Wraps HTMLAudioElement (or AudioContext-backed buffer when needed). Exposes `createAudioBus(parent)` returning `{ load(id, url), play(id, { volume?, rate? }), stop(id), dispose() }`. The engine doesn't yet need positional / 3D audio; this is the smallest primitive a project can bind to game events. SSR-safe (no-op without `globalThis.document`). Lives under `engine/runtime/audio/` and is reachable via `runtime.audio` so projects don't roll their own HTMLAudio singletons.
verification: engine/runtime/audio/audio-bus.ts ships createAudioBus + AudioBus type
unit test: load() + play() + stop() with mocked HTMLAudioElement; dispose() releases everything
runtime.audio is null in SSR / no-DOM contexts (graceful degradation)
engine/runtime/audio/audio-bus.ts ships createAudioBus + AudioBus type; lazy <audio> creation per id, restart-on-replay, autoplay rejection swallowed
RuntimeHandle.audio surfaces the bus; undefined when no document is available (SSR / Node) — projects branch with runtime.audio?.play(...)
+7 unit tests: SSR safety / load tracking / lazy create / unknown clip no-op / re-play same element / stop pause+rewind / dispose idempotent

stories.title: Kaboom Crew binds bomb / blast / pickup / death sounds
stories.summary: Project-local hook in `examples/kaboom-crew/bootstrap.ts` registers 4 clips on `runtime.audio` (bomb-place / blast / pickup / death) and a tiny system that emits the right call when it detects the corresponding event: `PlaceBombRequest` consumed → bomb-place; `BlastEvent` emitted → blast; `Pickup` removed by `PickupCollectSystem` → pickup; `BomberStats.alive` flips false → death. Clips ship as CC0 placeholders under `examples/kaboom-crew/assets/audio/`. The system stays project-local — engine audio surface remains generic.
verification: 4 clips registered + bound; live probe confirms `audio.play(...)` was invoked once per event (window.__agf.kaboom.audioLog mirror)
unit test: synthesised events drive the binding system through expected call sequence
createKaboomAudioBindingSystem detects bomb spawn / blast event / pickup removal / death via per-frame diffs; 6 unit tests cover each path + world swap reset

bootstrap registers the binding system BEFORE blast-propagation (so BlastEvent transient is visible) and threads onEvent into audio.play + audioLog mirror; runtime.kaboom.audioLog() exposes the event stream for probes

stories.title: Particle burst on each blast cell
stories.summary: When `BlastPropagationSystem` spawns a `BlastTile`, additionally spawn a short-lived ParticleEmitter entity tuned for an arcade explosion (orange/yellow, ~12 particles, 0.4 s lifetime). Re-uses the existing engine particle preset from M19. Single inline preset block in the system; no new engine work.
verification: snapshot after detonation shows N ParticleEmitter entities equal to the blast tile count
live probe screenshot: bomb at (3,3) produces a visible particle burst at the centre + each cardinal cell
BlastPropagationSystem spawns a ParticleEmitter entity (preset='spark', lifetime=0.4s, rate=30, maxParticles=12) alongside every BlastTile
M19 ParticleEmitter primitive handles particle lifetime — emitter cleans itself up after 0.4s; no new engine schema work

stories.title: Round counter + W/L tally in HUD
stories.summary: Promote the existing `RoundState` singleton with `roundNumber: number` and a `tally: { player: number, bot: number, draws: number }` block. `RoundResolveSystem` increments the right counter when the phase resolves; `restartScene` increments roundNumber and preserves the tally across the auto-restart wipe. The HUD `kaboom.stats` widget renders the new fields in a top row (e.g. `Round 7  •  W: 4  L: 2  D: 1`).
verification: unit test: phase=won bumps tally.player; phase=lost bumps tally.bot; phase=draw bumps tally.draws; counter survives scene.load
live probe: HUD stats widget includes 'Round N' + 'W:/L:/D:' after a kill
RoundState extended with roundNumber + tally; RoundResolveSystem bumps tally.player on 'won' / tally.bot on 'lost' / tally.draws on 'draw'; +3 unit tests
restartScene reads the previous RoundState before scene.load and re-creates kaboom.round-state with bumped roundNumber + preserved tally so the score survives the wipe
HUD stats widget now renders 'Round N   W:A L:B D:C' as the first row, then phase/timer/per-bomber stats below

stories.title: Title-screen overlay before the first round
stories.summary: Mount a centre-slot HUD widget on first boot showing 'Kaboom Crew — press Space to start'. The world stays paused (no bot AI / no input → bomb) until Space is pressed; on press, the widget unmounts and gameplay begins. Re-use the existing HUD center slot + the bootstrap's `_boundRestart` plumbing.
verification: live probe: after page load, HUD center shows 'press Space to start'; the bot bomber's GridPosition stays unchanged for ≥ 1.5 s
after a simulated Space keydown, the overlay disappears and bot.1 starts moving within ~0.5 s
scene-extensions schema: new GamePaused component (singleton marker on kaboom.game-state)
bot-ai / bomb-fuse / bomb-placement systems early-out when GamePaused is present on kaboom.game-state — no autonomous activity while the title screen is up
bootstrap creates the entity + marker on attachUi, mounts a center HUD overlay 'Kaboom Crew / Press SPACE to start', and consumes Space (first press) to remove the marker + dismiss the overlay

stories.title: Difficulty presets — easy / normal / hard
stories.summary: Project-level dial wired through the bootstrap. `?difficulty=easy|normal|hard` (default `normal`) maps to a tuple `{ aggression, decisionIntervalMs, range, speed }` applied to bot.1 at scene load. Easy: aggression 0.25 / 500 ms / range 2 / speed 2. Normal: 0.5 / 200 ms / 2 / 3. Hard: 0.85 / 120 ms / 3 / 4. The dial doesn't ship UI yet; query param is the agent-friendly contract.
verification: unit test: each preset assembles the expected component patch
live probe: loading ?difficulty=hard makes bot.1 move noticeably faster than ?difficulty=easy

examples/kaboom-crew/src/difficulty.ts exports the preset table, readDifficultyFromUrl + difficultyComponentPatch helpers; 5 unit tests cover narrowing / table values / URL fallback / preset honour / ms→seconds translation
bootstrap.attachUi applies the URL preset to bot.1 on first boot AND restartScene re-applies it on every round-restart so a query-param change survives the auto-reset

stories.title: examples/kaboom-crew/README.md — controls + agent surface + story
stories.summary: Short README under `examples/kaboom-crew/` covering the controls (WASD/arrows + Space + R), the agent surface (`window.__agf.kaboom.{gotoCell, placeBomb, status, restart}`), the round flow (start → bombs → blast → win/lose → auto-restart 3 s), the difficulty query param, and the file map (scenes / prefabs / src/systems / tests). One page; first paragraph is the player-facing pitch from SAMPLE_GAME_IDEAS.
verification: examples/kaboom-crew/README.md exists; engine check + repo:hygiene stay green
examples/kaboom-crew/README.md ships: pitch, controls table, run command + difficulty query param, round flow, HUD slot map, agent surface code samples, file map, determinism notes
repo:hygiene + check-no-console-log stay green; engine:check passes

stories.title: Deterministic 30-second recording fixture for regression replay
stories.summary: Capture a `Recording` of a 30-second seeded bot-vs-bot round and stash it under `examples/kaboom-crew/recordings/demo-30s.recording.json`. A new Vitest test replays the recording against a fresh world and asserts a stable final phase + RoundState.elapsed — gives the sprint a regression target for any AI / blast / pickup change, headless and fast (the existing playtest in S082 already shows the pattern).
verification: examples/kaboom-crew/recordings/demo-30s.recording.json exists; replay test passes
any change to BombFuseSystem / BlastPropagationSystem / BotAISystem that changes the determinism flips the test red

examples/kaboom-crew/tests/unit/demo-30s.test.ts boots the start scene, swaps player.1 to a BotBrain, runs 30 simulated seconds of the full system stack and snapshots { phase, tally, stoppedAtStep, bombers }

Vitest snapshot file under tests/unit/__snapshots__/demo-30s.test.ts.snap pins the deterministic outcome (phase='won', stoppedAtStep=336); re-run is byte-stable; any AI/blast/fuse change flips the diff

stories.title: engine doctor — Renderer probe section from rendererInspect()
stories.summary: Follow-on to S83 AGF-AGENT-RENDERER-PROBE. `engine doctor --renderer-inspect-from <path>` reads a snapshot dumped from `/__agf/renderer-inspect` and renders a 'Renderer:' section in the doctor report — info counters + handle leak callout + entity-id sample. Mirrors the AGF-LOG-DOCTOR-DIAGNOSTICS flag pattern.
verification: DoctorReport.rendererInspect populated from the supplied JSON; null otherwise
unit test: sample input → expected report shape (info.handleLeak + handles.entityIds.length surface in the report)

stories.title: Thread diagnostics bus into the renderer adapter (deferred from S83 audit)
stories.summary: S83 AUDIT-ENGINE marked the renderer files file-level allow-listed because the diagnostics bus didn't reach them. Thread the bus in: `ThreeRenderAdapter` constructor accepts `{ diagnostics? }`, and the three renderer-side warn/error sites (shader-patch fallback, async geometry/material binding failure, WebGPU adapter init fallback) migrate to `bus.emit({ severity, code: 'AGF_RENDER_*', ... })`. Adapter still keeps `console.warn` fallback when no bus is supplied (boot before the bus exists).
verification: ThreeRenderAdapter accepts diagnostics option; lifted call sites now emit through the bus when wired
smoke test confirms no renderer console output on a clean boot (the boot-noise-budget test stays green)

followUps: AGF-POOL-WARMUP-AND-UNIFY (next sprint) — user-reported pause on the first KABOOM-BLAST-PARTICLES burst points at the M19 particle pool growing under live demand. Story: pre-warm the particle pool to its default `maxParticles` cap on system register + unify the renderer pool surface (S48 render-pool-abstraction was left pending). Acceptance: a synthesised first-blast probe shows no frame-time spike vs steady-state, and InstancedMesh / BatchedMesh / Particle pools route through one registry.
